examples: In Britain, tabloid and even broadsheet newspapers monopolise coverage of sport.
Quality is representative of a culture which we Europeans have no reason to let others monopolise.
For once Rooney did not have to monopolise the stage as England played efficiently and ruthlessly.
A380 looks likely to dominate, if not monopolise, long high-density routes for years to come.
UN, despite its ability to monopolise the image of legitimacy, is ill-suited to administering territories in transition.
